card.title: MANTA AI
card.subtitle: RAG voice agent for aerospace · Team Lead · Oct–Nov 2025

interior.headline: 500 pages of technical manuals. Queryable in seconds, by voice.
interior.hook: MANTA Group supplies Leonardo and Boeing. Their operators work on the floor with 500+ pages of technical manuals — printed, indexed by hand, searched by memory. We had one month to change that.

sections.label: The problem
sections.body: Manual lookup on an aerospace manufacturing floor is slow, error-prone, and breaks flow. The information exists — it's just trapped in PDFs. The question was how to make it accessible in the way operators actually work: hands busy, eyes on the part, no time to type a search query.

sections.label: What we built
sections.body:
A functional RAG-based AI voice agent. Voiceflow for the voice layer, a RAG pipeline on top of the ingested manuals. Operators query 500+ pages of technical documentation via voice or text — and get an answer in seconds, not minutes. No new hardware required. No retraining. The knowledge base is the documentation MANTA already has.

Led a cross-university team of 4 (H-Farm College x Università di Puglia). Delivered a working MVP in a 1-month sprint. Pitched to a jury of representatives from 12 participating companies.

sections.label: Why it matters
sections.body: MANTA AI is not a portfolio exercise. It's a proof that AI-native tooling — voice + retrieval + a clean interface — can replace workflows that haven't changed in decades in industries that have never had a reason to change them. The same architecture scales to any knowledge-intensive field where the person doing the work can't stop to type.

interior.credits: Cross-university team of 4. Challenge: Impact Puglia — H-Farm College x Università di Puglia.

visual_identity.rationale: Dark, technical palette — aerospace context. Blue accent echoes industrial precision. Monochrome sans-serif throughout — this is a build piece, not a narrative piece.

hero.alt: MANTA AI — RAG voice agent architecture
hero.caption: RAG pipeline on 500+ pages of technical documentation, queryable by voice in seconds.
